name: Matrika
description: The Matrikas are a group of mother goddesses in Hinduism. They are fierce protectors and are often depicted as embodying the shakti, or energy, of different gods. The number of Matrikas varies depending on the tradition, ranging from seven (Saptamatrika) to sixteen. They are believed to have originated from the energies of the major gods during battles against demons, each representing a specific aspect of divine power. They are powerful and protective figures, often associated with fertility, motherhood, and the protection of children. While generally benevolent, they can also be fierce and destructive towards evil forces. Their worship is widespread across India, and they are invoked for protection, prosperity, and well-being. Common representations include Brahmani (Shakti of Brahma), Maheshwari (Shakti of Shiva), Kaumari (Shakti of Kartikeya), Vaishnavi (Shakti of Vishnu), Varahi (Shakti of Varaha/Vishnu), Indrani (Shakti of Indra), and Chamunda (Shakti of Durga or Kali). They are often depicted with weapons and riding their respective animal mounts, reflecting their association with the gods from whom they emanate. They are also connected to tantric practices and are considered powerful guardians against negative energies and diseases. The Matrikas, therefore, represent a complex interplay of maternal protectiveness and fierce power within the Hindu pantheon, showcasing the multifaceted nature of divine feminine energy.
subtitle: Divine Mothers, Fierce Protectors

keyMyths.title: Origin from Gods' Energies
keyMyths.description: The Matrikas are said to have originated from the energies of the major gods during battles against demons. Each Matrika embodies the power and essence of her respective male deity.

keyMyths.title: Protection of Children
keyMyths.description: The Matrikas are often invoked for the protection of children from diseases and negative influences. They are considered benevolent mothers who safeguard the young.

keyMyths.title: Association with Tantra
keyMyths.description: The Matrikas are significant figures in tantric traditions, representing powerful forces that can be harnessed for spiritual growth and protection. Their worship is often associated with rituals and mantras.
